title: Sussex: Hove, Washington Campsite, Bramber Castle & Brighton

main_route.excerpt: on Saturday at 9 am we set off for Sussex. We left Tonic [?at] kennels to be plucked, & continued on to the Gravestocks in Hove. Jonathan, Charlotte, Abigail & Tom gave us a delicious lunch.
Passing Lancing College, we came to Steyning — v. pretty, but only one small hut with one loo & cold water basin, so we drove on to Washington
so we drove on to Washington lovely clean modern loo block, continental, infra-red light comes on as you walk in. Expensive. Foot paths.

main_route.inference_reason: All trips start and end at home unless otherwise stated; no further diary pages for this trip

excursions.label: Day trip from Washington campsite: Bramber Castle and Brighton

stops.excerpt: We sat around the campsite until 11.30, then stopped at Bramber Castle, an old Norman castle, steep hill sides, outer wall & keep hill, not much left standing, except a few flint walls. Pretty.
On to the sea front where we had lunch. A white car got bogged down in the shingle.
The Pavillion was absolutely marvellous. Weird & fantastical, dragons, gold, Indian on the outside & Chinese on the inside. 1810. Oh George IV, while Prince Regent.
After this we went out on Brighton Pier, where all 5 boys went on Dodgems. John went on a ghastly high spinning machine too. The sea was a beautiful aquamarine colour. Windy.

unresolved.issue: Name of the kennels where Tinder the dog was left is unclear; 'Tonic' may be a mishearing or misread of the kennel name
unresolved.excerpt: We left Tonic [?at] kennels to be plucked

boundary_flags: mixed_end: true — the trip end is not clearly marked; diary continues onto A_0264_R which shows a postcard of the Royal Pavilion; return home is inferred
summary: An Easter weekend trip (April 10-11, 1999) to Sussex, visiting friends the Gravestocks in Hove for lunch before camping at Washington campsite in West Sussex. On the second day the family made a day trip to Bramber Castle and Brighton, visiting the Royal Pavilion and Brighton Pier before returning to camp for a pub supper.
extraction_notes: Trip is entirely domestic (UK only), no ferry crossings. The dog Tinder was left at kennels. The diary entry is incomplete at the end (mixed_end), with A_0264_R showing only a pasted postcard/print of the Royal Pavilion kitchen; the return to Birmingham is inferred. Steyning was considered as a campsite stop but rejected in favour of Washington. 'Salton' in the sketch caption on A_0263_R appears to refer to the Salton/Saltoun dragon motif on the Brighton Pavilion palm pillar — it is not a separate place visited. Page numbers visible are 7 (A_0263_R) and 8 (A_0264_R), suggesting this is near the end of a diary volume section. The date_end may extend beyond April 11 if the family stayed a third night (the diary says 'back to the campsite for a pub supper' but no further narrative follows), but no evidence confirms a third night.
